page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, the instance contains 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the c3-highmem-8 in the average tier across most workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1706.0 and a multi-core score of 6586.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 544.59 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 51.18 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 32.03 GB/sec for large-block writes. The system demonstrates strong database capabilities with 8.86 million Redis SET operations per second and a Passmark database operations score of 4055.25.

While the instance excels in memory-intensive tasks, it exhibits performance limitations in heavy computational tasks. For instance, LLM text generation using a medium 7B model drops to a weak 7.34 tokens/sec, and large 70B model inference is unsupported due to resource constraints. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and solid memory bandwidth, this instance is highly suited for memory-cached databases, medium-scale Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise applications rather than large-scale AI inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 4 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate balanced performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6586.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.86 million operations per second. While memory bandwidth is robust at 544.59 GB/sec for cached reads, the instance shows limitations in complex workloads, such as a weak 7.34 tokens/sec in medium 7B LLM text generation. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for database operations and memory-intensive applications.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core for memory-bound tasks
Delivers 8.86 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications
